Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze the Request Input Database schema PostgreSQL sample data for order_event_log orders and resources and a defined Assumed Normal Process Flow Task The user wants me to act as the Target LLM based on the provided prompt instructions Specifically I need to Identify anomalies and undesirable behaviors in the event log data compared to the normal process flow Hypothesize reasons for these anomalies Propose SQL queries to investigate these hypotheses further Constraint I must not provide hints or guidance to myself since I am acting as the target model responding to the prompt Goal Produce a comprehensive analysis report including anomalies hypotheses and SQL queries

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Data vs Normal Process Flow Normal Flow Register Order Perform Credit Check Validate Stock Confirm Shipment Ship Goods Issue Invoice Receive Payment

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 1001 Register Order Perform Credit Check Validate Stock Confirm Shipment Ship Goods Issue Invoice Receive Payment Analysis Follows the normal flow exactly No obvious anomalies

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1002 Register Order Confirm Shipment Expected Credit Check Anomaly Out of Order Ship Goods Expected Validate Stock Anomaly Out of Order Perform Credit Check Late Anomaly Control Failure Validate Stock Late Anomaly Control Failure Issue Invoice Receive Payment Analysis Major process violation Shipment confirmed and goods shipped before credit check and stock validation This is high risk shipping to uncredited customers shipping without stock check

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1003 Register Order Perform Credit Check Ship Goods Expected Validate Stock Confirm Shipment Anomaly Skipping Steps Out of Order Issue Invoice Confirm Shipment After Shipping Anomaly Logical Impossibility Retroactive Entry Receive Payment Analysis Goods shipped before shipment confirmation and potentially before stock validation missing step entirely Confirmation happens after shipping

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 1004 Register Order Receive Payment Expected Credit Check Anomaly Payment before Credit Invoice Issue Invoice After Payment Anomaly Billing Process Violation Confirm Shipment shipment_scheduled N Anomaly Confirmation without intent Ship Goods Analysis Payment received before invoice issued possible but unusual for standard B2B Missing Credit Check Missing Stock Validation Shipment confirmed as N but goods shipped later

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomaly 1 Sequence Violations Out of Order Activities Cases 1002 1003 1004 deviate significantly from the standard 1 7 flow

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis Emergency overrides system bypasses manual data entry errors backdating or lack of enforcement controls in the workflow engine

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomaly 2 Missing Activities Case 1003 missing Validate Stock Case 1004 missing Perform Credit Check and Validate Stock

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Hypothesis Steps skipped due to priority status Case 1002 is priority trusted customer lists or process negligence

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomaly 3 Logical Inconsistencies Case 1003 Ship Goods before Confirm Shipment Case 1004 Confirm Shipment has additional_info shipment_scheduled N but Ship Goods occurs later

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Hypothesis Data entry errors status updates not reflecting reality or Confirm Shipment activity used differently than intended

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Anomaly 4 Financial Control Risks Case 1002 Shipping before credit check Case 1004 Payment before Invoice Credit Check

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Hypothesis Fraud risk cash flow manipulation or specific customer agreements prepayment not captured in order_type

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Draft SQL Queries Need to detect sequence violations Need to detect missing steps Need to join with orders to see if order_type correlates with violations Need to join with resources to see if specific roles departments are causing violations

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Query 1 Sequence Violation Detection Compare timestamps of specific activity pairs for example Ship Goods vs Credit Check Query 2 Missing Steps Count expected activities per case_id Query 3 Resource Department Analysis Join with resources to find who is performing out of order actions Query 4 Order Type Correlation Join with orders to see if priority orders skip steps

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Refine Output Structure Introduction acknowledging the task Section 1 Identified Anomalies Detailed breakdown per case or type Section 2 Hypotheses for Root Causes Section 3 Proposed SQL Queries with explanations Conclusion Recommendations

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Self Correction Review during drafting Check Did I use the correct table names Yes order_event_log orders resources Check Did I use the correct column names Yes case_id activity timestamp etc Check Are the SQL queries valid PostgreSQL syntax Yes window functions CTEs joins Check Did I avoid giving hints to myself Yes I am writing the final response Check Ensure the distinction between resource in order_event_log and resource_id in resources is handled correctly in joins

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Final Polish Ensure tone is analytical and professional Make sure the SQL queries are robust enough to handle the general case not just the sample data Query Idea for Sequence Use LAG or self join to compare timestamps of specific activities Query Idea for Missing Steps Use COUNT distinct activities per case_id compared to expected count Query Idea for Resources Join order_event_log with resources on resource equals resource_id Lets assemble the response
